# unifi Provider

Manage a self-hosted UniFi Network controller (UniFi OS, Network >= 10.1.78) through the official Integration API with an X-API-KEY.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    unifi = {
      source = "PjSalty/unifi"
    }
  }
}

provider "unifi" {
  api_url        = "https://unifi.example.com" # or UNIFI_API
  api_key        = var.unifi_api_key           # or UNIFI_API_KEY
  site           = "default"                   # or UNIFI_SITE
  allow_insecure = true                        # self-signed console cert; or UNIFI_INSECURE
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `allow_insecure` (Boolean) Skip TLS verification for the console's self-signed certificate. Env: UNIFI_INSECURE.
- `api_key` (String, Sensitive) Integration API key sent as X-API-KEY. Env: UNIFI_API_KEY. Username/password is not supported by the official API.
- `api_url` (String) Base URL of the UniFi OS console. https only (e.g. https://unifi.example.com). Env: UNIFI_API.
- `destroy_protection` (Boolean) Block delete operations (safety rail). Env: UNIFI_DESTROY_PROTECTION.
- `read_only` (Boolean) Block all create/update/delete operations (safety rail). Env: UNIFI_READ_ONLY.
- `site` (String) Site to manage, resolved to its UUID. Defaults to "default". Env: UNIFI_SITE.
